nodes.panel_description: A rain-lashed road descends toward a timber checkpoint. Refugees wait between broken wagons; black pennants hang without wind.
nodes.text: Home is three miles away. The king's soldiers have placed a wall between you and it.

choices.text: Join the line and keep your head down.

nodes.panel_description: Seen from a muddy rise: six soldiers, a clerk, two cages, and a narrow drainage ditch beside the palisade.
nodes.speaker: Garren
nodes.text: Six men pretending there are no graves behind the trees. One of them keeps watching the merchant's wagon.

nodes.panel_description: Boots and wagon wheels churn the road to black paste. Beneath a canvas merchant wagon, one pale hand grips the axle.
nodes.speaker: Deserter
nodes.text: Vale. Don't look at me. Red Hollow was no ambush. They knew.

choices.text: You have mistaken me for someone else.

nodes.panel_description: Spears pull the deserter from under the wagon. No face is clear in the rain; only the clerk's white page catches the light.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: A useful pair of eyes. The army remembers useful men.

choices.text: Ask what they are searching for.

nodes.panel_description: Soldiers thrust spear shafts through hanging canvas while the merchant stares at the flooded ditch. A shape lies motionless beneath the sacks.
nodes.speaker: Tavin
nodes.text: The orders had the Chancellor's seal. Your commander kept a copy.

choices.text: Distract the patrol with the sealed letter.

nodes.panel_description: A silver military clasp changes hands in the shadow below the wagon. Beyond it, the checkpoint gate begins to close.
nodes.speaker: Tavin
nodes.text: Take it. But when they ask who carried Captain Orl's letter, remember what you bought.

nodes.panel_description: The wagon recedes behind a curtain of rain. A shout rises, followed by the dull certainty of men who have found something.
nodes.text: You make it through the gate. Behind you, someone says your name.

nodes.panel_description: Under the checkpoint awning, wet belongings are spread across a scarred table. The sealed letter sits between a knife and an ink pot.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: A dead commander's seal. That makes this property of the Crown.

choices.text: Claim it contains names of deserters.

nodes.panel_description: The merchant's wagon passes between sharpened stakes as a wheel sinks deep. Everyone must push; no one can remain hidden.
nodes.speaker: Sella Venn
nodes.text: If your friend wants to live, he had better become useful quickly.

nodes.panel_description: Reeds bow beneath running water outside the palisade. Two distant figures crawl through the flooded ditch while bells ring above.
nodes.speaker: Tavin
nodes.text: They call me Tavin. If we live through this, I will tell you who signed the order.

choices.text: Keep moving toward the ruined chapel.

nodes.panel_description: The road is empty except for the deserter kneeling beside the wagon and a ring of leveled spears.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: Changed your mind, Vale? Men usually do when the cost becomes visible.

choices.text: Surrender the letter for his life.

nodes.panel_description: Meret breaks the outer wax but pauses at the second, hidden seal. Rainwater crawls across the names on her ledger.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: Then you will not object to reading those names aloud.

nodes.panel_description: The gate slams shut. A bell rings once, then stops as if someone has strangled it. Soldiers search every wagon while the clerk rewrites the same order.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: No one leaves until I know which part of this mess belongs to the Crown and which part belongs to the dead.

choices.text: Ask what happened at Red Hollow.
Find Sella before the soldiers do.
Look for the order behind the order.

nodes.panel_description: Captain Meret stands beneath a leaking awning. Her gloves are clean; the rest of her is not.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: Captain Orl wrote your name beside the prisoners. That usually means a man was trusted, feared, or both.

choices.text: Tell her Orl was afraid of the Crown.
Refuse to give her a dead man's thoughts.

nodes.panel_description: A ration ledger lies open beside the checkpoint map. Several units were fed for three days after their names were crossed out.
nodes.speaker: Garren
nodes.text: The dead were still being counted. Someone needed them hungry in the right place.

choices.text: Copy the seal and the unit numbers.
Put the ledger back and remember the clerk.

nodes.panel_description: Sella blocks the wagon with one hand on the reins. Behind her, a child coughs under a wool blanket while soldiers turn over sacks of flour.
nodes.speaker: Sella Venn
nodes.text: You brought the rain, the soldiers, and a wanted man. Tell me which one I am meant to invoice.

choices.text: Promise to get her wagon through.
Tell her Tavin is no longer her problem.
Ask what she was carrying before the flour.

nodes.panel_description: The wagon's false floor is exposed by a broken wheel. Beneath the flour are ration marks, a child's shoe, and a strip of stamped leather.
nodes.speaker: Sella Venn
nodes.text: Everyone searches for treasure. Mostly they find proof that someone poorer was here first.

nodes.panel_description: Tavin waits beside the drainage ditch, soaked through and smiling like a man who has misplaced his last good idea.
nodes.speaker: Tavin
nodes.text: The order carried the Chancellor's seal. Your commander kept the copy because he meant to sell the truth, not die for it.

choices.text: Believe the useful part and doubt the rest.
Threaten him until the story improves.
Offer him to Meret for a clean passage.

nodes.panel_description: Meret folds the search order into a square small enough to swallow. Her horse stamps at the mud as if it has somewhere better to be.
nodes.speaker: Captain Meret
nodes.text: Give me the deserter and the letter. I give you a gate, a room, and a story in which you were never here.

choices.text: Ask what happens to Sella's family.

nodes.panel_description: The copied order and the sealed letter are held apart on a table between three wet hands. One seal is official. The other is only trying to look it.
nodes.speaker: Garren
nodes.text: The regiment was not sent to win. It was sent to make a story believable.

choices.text: Take the evidence to the ruined chapel.
Keep the evidence hidden and leave alone.

nodes.panel_description: The checkpoint gate opens. Behind it, a cage creaks as the road climbs toward home.
nodes.text: You pass as a loyal soldier. By sunset, the Crown knows you have returned.

nodes.panel_description: A royal courier rides east with the letter beneath his coat. Garren walks west beneath bare trees.
nodes.text: The truth is safe in the hands of the people who buried it once already.

nodes.panel_description: Black scraps rise from the brazier and vanish into rain. Soldiers and refugees watch the same empty sky.
nodes.text: No one can use the letter now. No one can prove what happened at Red Hollow.

nodes.panel_description: The road beyond the checkpoint winds through drowned fields toward a town without bells.
nodes.speaker: Garren
nodes.text: Survival is only courage told by the survivor.

nodes.panel_description: A ruined chapel stands above the marsh. Two small figures approach while torchlight searches the road behind them.
nodes.text: The deserter lives, the letter remains sealed, and somebody powerful has learned to be afraid.

nodes.panel_description: The checkpoint remains shut behind Garren. Beyond the ditch, a broken chapel stands above the marsh, its bell rope hanging like a severed vein.
nodes.speaker: Garren
nodes.text: The gate has not opened. It has only decided who is worth stopping next.

choices.text: Let the Crown carry the letter.
Leave everyone else to the soldiers.
